content: We can frame Self-Refine’s iterative unguided refinement framework as a 'base chassis' that can be modified to integrate multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refinement system that still retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data. This combined system would leverage the tension between inconsistent solving perspectives to catch hidden flaws in the LLM’s initial output that single-perspective Self-Refine would miss, while avoiding the overhead of external supervision.
